title: Trump denies conflict of interest over crypto. And, Vatican excommunicates rebel group
content:
# 国际新闻英文阅读

## 新闻标题
Trump denies conflict of interest over crypto. And, Vatican excommunicates rebel group

## 新闻背景
本期新闻涵盖多个国际事件：美国总统特朗普的财务披露显示其家族通过加密货币等商业活动获利超10亿美元，引发利益冲突争议；梵蒂冈正式将保守派团体“圣庇护十世司铎兄弟会”逐出教会，因其未经教皇批准任命主教；委内瑞拉遭受罕见双地震袭击，死亡人数已超2200人；美国男足在世界杯中2-0击败波黑，自2002年以来首次晋级淘汰赛。此外，还包括食物浪费、自闭症儿童游泳安全等社会话题。

## 英文原文
President Trump's financial disclosures reveal that he and his family earned more than $1 billion through cryptocurrency ventures and other businesses last year, according to a 927-page report filed with the Office of Government Ethics. The report shows that more than $500 million came from the cryptocurrency venture "World Liberty Financial," which was co-founded by Trump family members. The sale of souvenir "meme" coins featuring Trump's image generated more than $600 million. Other income included more than $50 million from settlements with media companies and millions in profits from Trump-branded products like Bibles, sneakers and watches. These earnings, which have outpaced his real estate business, have sparked concerns about potential conflicts of interest. The White House released a statement denying any conflicts of interest, and spokesperson Anna Kelly applauded Trump for making the U.S. "the crypto capital of the world."

The Vatican this morning formalized the excommunications of the bishops and priests of the conservative group known as the Society of St. Pius X, declaring that it has entered schism and broken communion with the pope and the Catholic Church. The group, known as SSPX, celebrates the traditional Latin Mass and opposes some modern church reforms. In the Catholic Church, the appointment of new bishops is the responsibility of the pope. But yesterday, the group defied Pope Leo XIV by consecrating four bishops without his approval. The Society framed its actions as a defense of Catholic tradition. During the ceremony, the Rev. Davide Pagliarani, head of the Society of St. Pius X, described the consecrations as an act of service rather than rebellion. Two of the excommunicated men teach in the U.S., where the group's membership has been growing, according to the society.

A little over a week has passed since rare double earthquakes struck Venezuela. Thousands of people are feared dead as the official death toll continues to rise and hope diminishes for finding survivors in the rubble. Yesterday, the number of people killed by the quakes reached 2,295, and more than 11,200 people were injured, said Jorge Rodríguez, the president of Venezuela's National Assembly. Tens of thousands of people remain unaccounted for. The number of people left homeless could be staggering. An analysis of satellite data by Corey Scher and Jamon Van Den Hoek from Oregon State University estimated that 58,870 buildings were likely damaged or destroyed by the earthquakes. The U.N.'s International Organization for Migration has reported that up to 6.8 million people could be affected by the disaster, needing shelter, water, sanitation, healthcare and other relief items. Here are the most significant developments since the tragedy occurred.

The U.S. men's national team defeated Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0, marking its first FIFA World Cup knockout round victory since 2002. With this win, the U.S. advances to the Round of 16, where it will face Belgium on July 6. The team will have to play without U.S. forward Folarin Balogun, who will be suspended after receiving a red card for a cleat-first challenge on Bosnian defender Tarik Muharemović. Balogun has scored three goals in three World Cup matches, including the first goal of yesterday's game. Balogun is currently the top goalscorer for the U.S. team in the 2026 World Cup.

Don't let your leftovers go to waste. According to the nonprofit ReFED, about a quarter of all food products are wasted nationally. Life Kit spoke with chefs who shared clever storage techniques and cooking tips to help you make the most of your food scraps.

Children with autism are 160 times more likely to die from drowning compared to their peers, according to a 2017 study from Columbia University. This is largely because about half of autistic children have a tendency to wander away from safe environments. That fact, combined with an attraction to water, can be very dangerous. High-quality swim lessons can be a vital preventative measure to combat this issue. Florida, which has one of the highest childhood drowning rates in the nation, expanded a voucher program this month that puts children ages 1-7 who have autism at the front of the line for subsidized swim lessons. The YMCA of South Florida's low-cost Swim Buddies program is one of the largest programs specifically designed for children with disabilities in the area. One of its key features is its focus on individualization and one-on-one instruction, rather than traditional group lessons. Staff members assess each child's needs and tailor the lessons accordingly.

## 高频词汇学习

- **cryptocurrency** [ˌkrɪptoʊˈkɜːrənsi]
  - 名词
  - 加密货币
  - 原文例句：President Trump's financial disclosures reveal that he and his family earned more than $1 billion through cryptocurrency ventures...
  - 新闻语境用法：指基于区块链技术的数字货币，如比特币、以太坊等。在政治新闻中常与金融监管、利益冲突等话题关联。

- **excommunication** [ˌekskəˌmjuːnɪˈkeɪʃən]
  - 名词
  - 逐出教会；开除教籍
  - 原文例句：The Vatican this morning formalized the excommunications of the bishops and priests of the conservative group...
  - 新闻语境用法：天主教会的最高惩罚，指将某人排除在教会圣事和团体之外。常用于宗教冲突或教义争议报道中。

- **consecrate** [ˈkɑːnsɪkreɪt]
  - 动词
  - 祝圣；奉献；任命（主教）
  - 原文例句：the group defied Pope Leo XIV by consecrating four bishops without his approval.
  - 新闻语境用法：在宗教语境中指通过正式仪式任命主教或使某物神圣化。此处强调未经授权的任命行为。

- **schism** [ˈskɪzəm]
  - 名词
  - 分裂；教会分立
  - 原文例句：declaring that it has entered schism and broken communion with the pope...
  - 新闻语境用法：指一个宗教团体内部的分裂，通常涉及教义或权威的争议。在教会新闻中是一个严重指控。

- **death toll** [deθ toʊl]
  - 名词
  - 死亡人数
  - 原文例句：the official death toll continues to rise and hope diminishes for finding survivors in the rubble.
  - 新闻语境用法：灾难报道中的核心数据，通常随救援进展而更新。常与“rise, climb, reach”等动词搭配。

- **unaccounted for** [ˌʌnəˈkaʊntɪd fɔːr]
  - 形容词短语
  - 下落不明的；未查明的
  - 原文例句：Tens of thousands of people remain unaccounted for.
  - 新闻语境用法：常用于灾难报道中指尚未确认生死的失踪人员，比“missing”更强调统计上的不确定性。

- **sanitation** [ˌsænɪˈteɪʃən]
  - 名词
  - 卫生设施；公共卫生
  - 原文例句：up to 6.8 million people could be affected by the disaster, needing shelter, water, sanitation, healthcare...
  - 新闻语境用法：人道主义援助中的关键领域，包括排污、垃圾处理、清洁饮水等。常与“shelter, water, healthcare”并列出现。

- **knockout round** [ˈnɑːkaʊt raʊnd]
  - 名词
  - 淘汰赛阶段
  - 原文例句：marking its first FIFA World Cup knockout round victory since 2002.
  - 新闻语境用法：体育赛事术语，指小组赛后的淘汰制比赛阶段。与之相对的是“group stage”（小组赛）。

- **red card** [red kɑːrd]
  - 名词
  - 红牌（判罚）
  - 原文例句：who will be suspended after receiving a red card for a cleat-first challenge...
  - 新闻语境用法：足球比赛中最严重的个人处罚，意味着球员被罚出场并可能面临后续禁赛。常用于体育报道。

- **voucher** [ˈvaʊtʃər]
  - 名词
  - 代金券；补贴券
  - 原文例句：Florida expanded a voucher program this month that puts children ages 1-7 who have autism at the front of the line for subsidized swim lessons.
  - 新闻语境用法：政府或机构发放的用于特定服务的补贴凭证。在政策报道中常见于教育、医疗、住房等领域。

## 重点表达解析

- **conflict of interest**
  - 含义：利益冲突（指个人利益与职责之间可能存在的矛盾）
  - 使用场景：政治、商业、法律报道中，当官员或公司高管的行为可能为个人带来不当利益时使用
  - 例句：The senator resigned after an investigation revealed a conflict of interest involving his wife's company.

- **frame something as**
  - 含义：将某事描述/界定为（某种性质）
  - 使用场景：新闻中用于描述某人如何为自己的行为辩护或赋予特定意义
  - 例句：The company framed the layoffs as a necessary restructuring rather than a cost-cutting measure.

- **make the most of**
  - 含义：充分利用；善加利用
  - 使用场景：生活建议、商业策略、资源管理等语境
  - 例句：With limited time before the exam, she tried to make the most of every study session.

- **have a tendency to**
  - 含义：有……的倾向；往往
  - 使用场景：描述行为模式、性格特征或统计趋势
  - 例句：Stock markets have a tendency to overreact to short-term news.

- **tailor something to**
  - 含义：根据……调整/定制（使适合特定需求）
  - 使用场景：教育、医疗、服务设计等需要个性化方案的领域
  - 例句：The teacher tailored the curriculum to meet the needs of students with different learning styles.

## 阅读理解练习

1. 根据原文，特朗普家族的加密货币企业“World Liberty Financial”在去年产生了多少收入？
   A. 超过10亿美元
   B. 超过6亿美元
   C. 超过5亿美元
   D. 超过50亿美元

   答案：C

2. 梵蒂冈将圣庇护十世司铎兄弟会逐出教会的主要原因是什么？
   A. 该团体拒绝使用拉丁语进行弥撒
   B. 该团体未经教皇批准任命了四位主教
   C. 该团体在批评现代教会改革
   D. 该团体在美国的成员数量在减少

   答案：B

3. 根据原文，佛罗里达州推出的游泳课程代金券项目有何特点？
   A. 对所有1-7岁儿童开放申请
   B. 为自闭症儿童提供优先名额和补贴
   C. 只提供传统的小组课程
   D. 由哥伦比亚大学的研究团队管理

   答案：B
